title: Nursery Agent Specification
description: Declarative specification for a Nursery agent. One spec describes one reproducible agent. See https://github.com/ZetiMente/nursery for context.

name.description: Unique identifier for this agent on its host. Used for container naming, routing, and logs.

image.description: Container image the agent runs in. Typically a Nursery base image plus any agent-specific layers.

model.description: Model identifier in the form <provider>/<model>. The container translates this to a real inference client at runtime.

host.description: Which host runtime the agent is designed to run on. Selects the default image variant and gateway conventions. Override at spawn time with --host.

soul.description: Path to the agent's persona file (typically SOUL.md). Resolved relative to the spec file.

workspace.description: Path on the host where the agent's persistent workspace lives. Mounted into the container. This is the agent's identity — it survives respawns.

secrets.description: Named secrets to mount into the container at runtime. Resolved by the host to files under /run/secrets/ inside the agent. Agents reference secrets by name; values never live in the spec.

channels.description: Channels the agent connects to (e.g. telegram, discord, signal). The host gateway handles routing.

capabilities.description: Capabilities the agent may exercise (e.g. gmail, calendar, tasks). Enforced by the host adapter, not the agent itself.

resources.description: Optional resource limits passed to the container runtime.

environment.description: Optional non-secret environment variables passed to the agent at runtime. Do NOT put secrets here — use the 'secrets' field instead.

labels.description: Optional key/value metadata. Not interpreted by Nursery itself — purely for the operator's bookkeeping.
